problem: How to engineer materials that autonomously repair microcracks before failure?
mechanism: Two-dimensional material stacking (van der Waals heterostructure)
hypothesis: The key to How to engineer materials that autonomously repair microcracks before failure? is Two-dimensional material stacking (van der Waals heterostructure) — specifically Two-dimensional material stacking — which would help 1,000,000,000 people affected.

experiment.protocol: Mechanical testing (tensile, fatigue) with 30 month follow-up. Control group receives placebo/sham.

wisdom: Nature uses only the longest threads to weave her patterns. — Richard Feynman
